chapter_name: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom
subject: Old - English
note: Practice feed me correct answer nahi hota; jaha ID important-questions se match hua waha answer bhara hai (answer_source).

questions.question_text: How was the old couple projected to be in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.solution: The old couple in the story was very honest and hardworking. The kind couple loved their pet dog a lot and was generous towards all.

questions.question_text: Which animal was kept as a pet by the old couple in the plot, The ashes that made the trees bloom?
questions.solution: The old couple had a little dog as their pet. The couple was very fond of it and took good care of the dog.

questions.question_text: What was the name of the old couple's pet dog? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: Muko was the name given by the old couple to their pet dog. The couple had no children, so, they loved Muko as though it was a baby.

questions.question_text: Who followed the old man to work every day in the plot, The ashes made trees bloom?
questions.solution: Every day the dog followed his master, a rice farmer to his work, into the fields where the old man worked hard from morning till the sunset.

questions.question_text: What did the old man find as he struck his hoe in the earth in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.solution: The dog convinced and made his master dig a particular spot in the ground and as the old man struck his hoe in the earth, a pile of gold gleamed before him.

questions.question_text: What did the dog do on reaching the spot where the treasure was hidden? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: The dog began a lively scratching on the spot where it had managed to make it's master follow too because of its actions. The dog hinted the old man of something buried inside the ground on that particular spot by scratching it.

questions.question_text: What did the old couple do after they found the pile of gold? (The ashes that made trees bloom)

options.label: A
options.text: They bought a piece of land

options.label: C
options.text: They gave plentifully to their poor neighbours

options.label: D
options.text: They made a feast for their friends

questions.solution: The old couple was very kind and generous, so, with their new-found treasure, they purchased a piece of land and then made a feast for their friends and distributed generously to their poor neighbours.

questions.question_text: How was the other couple that lived in the village where the old man and his wife lived? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: In the same village, there lived a wicked old man and his wife who were very greedy and insensitive to others' needs.

questions.question_text: What did the cruel couple do to Muko in hope that it would find a treasure for them?

options.label: A
options.text: They set other dainties before it

options.label: C
options.text: They coaxed the dog into their garden

options.label: D
options.text: They set bits of fish before it

questions.solution: The wicked couple coaxed the dog into their garden and set bits of fish and other dainties before him to lure him to find the treasure for them.

questions.question_text: How did Muko feel when the wicked couple tried to lure him with good food?
questions.solution: The dog was scared of the cruel couple, so, it neither moved nor ate the dainties offered to it by them.

questions.question_text: What did the cruel couple find beneath the surface of digging? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: The wicked old man began to dig but unfortunately found nothing but a dead kitten buried near the pine tree in the garden.

questions.question_text: What was the reaction of the cruel old couple on seeing the dead kitten in the plot, the ashes that made trees bloom?
questions.solution: The wicked couple on seeing the dead kitten got enraged as they expected some treasure instead. The old man started thrashing the poor animal and his wicked wife chopped the dog's head out of rage.

questions.question_text: Who nearly chopped off the dog's head? (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: The wicked old woman who happened to be a neighbour of the dog's masters nearly chopped off the poor animal's head as it failed to help her in finding treasure.

questions.question_text: Why did the neighbours kill the dog in the plot, The ashes that made trees bloom?

options.label: A
options.text: They wanted to eat the dog

options.label: B
options.text: The dog had lost its mind

options.label: D
options.text: The dog failed to find the treasure for them

questions.correct_answer: The dog failed to find the treasure for them
questions.solution: The greedy old couple coaxed the dog into their garden and lured him with dainties, so, that the dog would help them to find treasure buried inside the garden and become rich. The dog failed to do so and in a fit of rage, they killed the poor animal mercilessly.

questions.question_text: What did the pastry and sauce turn into as the greedy couple began to pound and grind them?
questions.solution: The stingy and wicked couple borrowed the magic mill from their neighbour and began to pound and grind the rice and beans. But at the first blow and turn, the pastry and sauce turned into a foul mass of worms.

questions.question_text: When the pastry was ready:

options.label: B
options.text: the fireplace was clean and tidy

questions.solution: The farmer and his wife baked the pastry and suddenly the whole mass (it) changed into gold coins. When the old woman took the hand-mill and filling it with beans began to grind, the gold dropped like rain.

questions.question_text: The mill was turned ________.
questions.solution: The jealous old neighbour noticed the old couple getting rich again. So they also did the same. But their pastry and sauce turned into worms. They destroyed the mill borrowed from the old couple and burnt it. Thus, the mill was turned into ashes.

questions.question_text: Daimio was delighted to see the:
questions.solution: Daimio was delighted to see the cherry tree blossom. The delighted daimio ordered the train to be stopped and got out to see the wonder. He thanked the man, offered him presents and also invited him to the castle.

questions.question_text: The dog's master:
questions.solution: The good old man scattered the ashes over the withered cherry tree and suddenly it burst into blossom. The landlord got out to see the wonder. He thanked the man, offered him presents and also invited him to the castle.

questions.question_text: The greedy neighbour: (The ashes that made trees bloom)
questions.solution: When the greedy neighbour heard of the magic of the ashes he too tried to do the same. But the tree showed no change. The dust rather blew into the nose and eyes of his lordship. The man who was escorting the lord dragged the greedy man from the tree and threw him into the ditch. The greedy man was also beaten by them soundly and later died in the mud.

questions.question_text: Daimio was pleased when the old man sprinkled ash over the cherry tree because:
questions.solution: Daimio was pleased when the old man sprinkled ash over the cherry tree because the withered tree blossomed.

questions.question_text: The daimio invited the good old man ________.
questions.solution: The delighted daimio stopped and got out to see the wonder. He thanked the old man, offered him presents and also invited him to his castle.

questions.question_text: What is the story The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom about?

options.label: A
options.text: A quarrelsome couple and their greed

options.label: B
options.text: A clever thief and his dog

options.label: D
options.text: An honest person and his dog

questions.solution: The Ashes That Made Trees Bloom is a story that underscores values such as honesty, compassion, diligence, etc. with a hint of magical realism. It is a story about an honest and hardworking couple.

questions.question_text: What did the old couple have?
questions.solution: The old couple had a little pet dog named Muko. They loved it as if it were their baby.

questions.question_text: Why would the white heron walk in the footsteps of the old man in the field?
questions.solution: The white heron walked in the footsteps of the old man to pick up the worms.

questions.question_text: What did the spirit of the dog tell the old man to do?

options.label: A
options.text: To make a mill for the bean sauce

options.label: C
options.text: To cut down the pine over his grave

options.label: D
options.text: To make from it a mortar for rice pastry

questions.solution: At night the spirit of the dog appeared to the old man in a dream and said, “Cut down the pine tree over my grave, and make from it a mortar for your rice pastry and a mill for your bean sauce.”

questions.question_text: Where did the wicked neighbour stand to welcome diamio?
questions.solution: The greedy neighbour took some of the magic ashes and went out on the highway. There he waited until a daimio’s train came along.

questions.question_text: What happened to the wicked old man in the end?

options.label: C
options.text: Put to death by the daimio

questions.solution: The man who was escorting the lord (daimio) dragged the greedy man from the tree and threw him into the ditch. He also beat him soundly. The greedy man thus died in the mud.

questions.question_text: What happened when the wicked man sprinkled the ashes?
questions.solution: When the greedy neighbour heard that the old man's magic delighted the diamio, he also took the magic ashes to the highway. He waited until the landlord’s train came along, and instead of kneeling down like the crowd, he climbed a withered cherry tree. When the landlord was directly under him, he threw a handful of ashes over him. But the tree showed no change. The dust rather blew into the nose and eyes of his lordship.

questions.question_text: Where did the old couple live?
questions.solution: In the 19th century, Japan there lived an old couple. Their only companion was a little pet dog named Muko.

questions.question_text: How many children did the old couple have?
questions.solution: In the 19th century Japan there lived an old couple. Their only companion was a little pet dog named Muko. Having no children, they loved it as though it were a baby.

questions.question_text: Which crops did the old man grow in fields?
questions.solution: The old man was a rice farmer. He worked hard with his spade from morning till sunset. The dog followed him every day to the field.

questions.question_text: How did the dog convince the old man for digging the earth?
questions.solution: One day the dog came running to him, putting his paws against his legs and motioning with his head to some spot behind. The old man at first thought his pet was only playing then he followed the dog a few yards to a place where the animal began a lively scratching.

questions.question_text: The old man found ________.
questions.solution: When the old man struck his hoe in the earth a pile of gold gleamed before him.

questions.question_text: Who killed the dog?
questions.solution: When the wicked old couple dug the earth to get gold but found only a dead cat in the pit. They beat the dog to death in a fit of anger.

questions.question_text: What did the dog say to the old man in his dream?
questions.solution: The spirit of the dog said to cut down the pine tree over his grave to his master in his dream.

questions.question_text: What does the first paragraph mainly talk about? (The Ashes that Made Trees Bloom)

options.label: A
options.text: The relationship between the couple and the dog.

options.label: B
options.text: What the couple fed the dog.

options.label: C
options.text: The way the dog treated other animals.

options.label: D
options.text: Why the couple decided to keep a dog.

questions.correct_answer: The relationship between the couple and the dog.
questions.solution: The relationship between the couple and the dog.

questions.question_text: When the dog started running and whining, initially the man was ________.
questions.solution: Unconcerned

questions.question_text: Why did the cruel couple carry a spade and a hoe?

options.label: D
options.text: To dig up the dead cat

questions.solution: To dig for treasure

questions.question_text: What do the old man's actions on the night of his pet's death show?

options.label: B
options.text: He treated him like a family member.

options.label: C
options.text: He wished to hide his grief from others.

options.label: D
options.text: He did not believe that the dog had died.

questions.correct_answer: He treated him like a family member.
questions.solution: He treated him like a family member.

questions.question_text: Which adjective best describes the wicked couple's behaviour when they saw the old woman was grinding beans?
questions.solution: Prying

questions.question_text: What was the main purpose of the restrictions during the daimio's procession?

options.label: A
options.text: To test the loyalty of the people towards him.

options.label: B
options.text: To establish his superiority over common people.

options.label: C
options.text: To demonstrate his capability as a ruler.

options.label: D
options.text: To prove the daimio's concern for his subjects.

questions.correct_answer: To establish his superiority over common people.
questions.solution: To establish his superiority over common people.

questions.question_text: What does the shepherd's attitude towards the blanket show?

options.label: B
options.text: You have to act fast to get what you want.

options.label: C
options.text: Good things happen to those who are patient.

questions.solution: Your actions determine your rewards.
